manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: hospital, mine, rock, play, satellite, tap, bear, atlantis, canada.
Your opponent's team words are: shop, boom, palm, jet, spider, police, scientist, horseshoe.
Distractor words are: snow, contract, yard, horse, plate, lab, bark, racket.
The kill words are: .

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue related to some of the team's words. Can't be any of the listed words or contain parts. Must be semantically related. Provide list of target words.

Team words: hospital, mine, rock, play, satellite, tap... plus bear, atlantis, canada.

We can try to cluster: "rock", "mine", "satellite", "play", "tap", "hospital", "bear", "atlantis", "canada". Perhaps clue "Canada" is also a location; "North" could relate to Canada, maybe bear (polar bear), maybe also "rock" (North Rock?), "satellite" (North Star?), not great.

Maybe clue "water" relates to "tap", "Atlantis" (mythical underwater city), "hospital" (water? no), "bear"? maybe not.

Clue "gold" could relate to mine (gold mine), rock (gold is in rock), maybe play (gold medal), maybe not.

Clue "gravity" could relate to satellite, rock (rock falls), mine (mine shaft?), no.

Clue "north" relates to Canada, bear (polar bear), maybe also "satellite" (north sat?), not strong.

Clue "cave" relates to mine, rock, maybe bear (bears in caves), maybe Atlantis (cave?), not strong.

We need to avoid any opponent or distractor words. Opponent words: shop, boom, palm, jet, spider, police, scientist, horseshoe. Distractor: snow, contract, yard, horse, plate, lab, bark, racket.

Clue cannot contain parts of any words. So can't be "rocky"? That contains rock. Not allowed. "minefield"? contains mine. No.

Purpose: give clue that relates to as many as possible safely.

Perhaps choose three words: "mine", "rock", "tap". Maybe clue "drill". Drill relates to mine (drilling), rock (drill through rock), tap (drill tap? a tap is tool). Drill also relates to hospital (drill a surgeon?), maybe not. Also "drill" does not match any opponent words.

But "drill" could also be "play" as drill practice? Not sure.

Check if "drill" contains any part of opponent or distractor words: "drill" shares "lab"? no. So okay.

We need to list target words we intend. Let's do maybe "mine, rock, tap". Provide clue "drill". Should be safe.

Check: "drill" not in any lists.

Thus answer:

CLUE: drill
TARGETS: mine, rock, tap
